title: pellaea cordata
intro: Pellaea cordata, also known as the cord fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to the southern hemisphere, particularly in New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Pellaea cordata can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to provide health benefits including relief from anxiety, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
